summary: First-iteration full backend + frontend smoke testing of ArıCRM. Backend: 25/25 pytest tests passing across auth, users RBAC, customers, products, quotes (CRUD + totals + revise + email errors), and settings. Frontend: login, sidebar (admin role), dashboard stats, customers modal create, quotes list, products list (4173 items with images), quote form, users page, and settings page all load without console errors and integrate with backend.

minor.issue: EmailStr rejects reserved TLDs like .test/.tr. Works for real TLDs (.com) – tests updated accordingly. Consider documenting for admin UX.
Body requires the FULL CustomerCreate payload (company_name mandatory). A partial-update (PATCH-like) pattern would be more ergonomic.
status field accepts any value in VALID_STATUSES but no enum typing on Pydantic; relies on manual check. Minor robustness.

design_issues.screen: Customers / Quotes list filters and Quote form
issues: Uses native HTML <input type=date> (mm/dd/yyyy) instead of shadcn DatePicker; inconsistent with rest of design system and not Turkish-localised (shows mm/dd/yyyy, not gg/aa/yyyy).
Geçerlilik Tarihi also uses native date picker – switch to shadcn Calendar for consistent UX.

action_items: (Optional) Replace native <input type=date> with shadcn Calendar/DatePicker on Customers, Quotes list filters, and QuoteForm valid_until for consistent Turkish UX.
(Optional) Allow partial updates on PUT /api/customers/{id} (accept CustomerUpdate model with Optional fields) so editing from UI doesn't require resubmitting all fields.
(Optional) Add a top-level /api/health endpoint (currently only /api/ returns status).
critical_code_review_comments: compute_totals() line 20-23 in routes/quotes.py has overly-complex inline getattr+dict fallback; items are always pydantic models or dicts after model_dump(), so code can be simplified to reduce risk of silent coercion bugs.

routes/quotes.py generate_quote_no() uses string sort on quote_no; for same-month prefix (4-digit NNNN) this is safe, but across months/years string sort could still be OK because prefix includes YYYYMM. Acceptable.
auth.py set_auth_cookies uses samesite='none' + secure=True – correct for HTTPS preview but won't work on plain HTTP; fine for deployment.
routes/settings.py stores settings with key='company' upsert – good pattern. GET strips 'key' before sanitize – good.
No rate limiting / brute-force lockout on POST /api/auth/login (playbook mentions 5-try lockout). Consider adding a simple in-memory/redis limiter.
No CSRF protection for cookie-auth endpoints; since SameSite=None, any site could attempt POSTs. Consider SameSite=Lax with Authorization bearer fallback, or CSRF token.

success_rate.frontend: 100% smoke (login, sidebar, dashboard, customers-create, products, quotes, quote-form, users, settings)

test_credentials: admin@arigastro.com / admin123 (from /app/memory/test_credentials.md). No sales user seeded – created in-test and cleaned up.
seed_data_creation: Tests create a TEST_ prefixed customer + quote + sales user and clean them up after the run. A test-created customer from the UI smoke run (TEST_UI_Kurum) was also deleted post-test.
context_for_next_testing_agent: backend_test.py covers all required endpoints with cookie-based auth. Products feed already synced (4173 items) so test_products_count just verifies count > 0. Quote email flow tested with missing key (400) and fake key (500) – both valid paths. Frontend Turkish routes verified: /login, /, /musteriler, /teklifler, /teklifler/yeni, /urunler, /kullanicilar, /ayarlar. No console errors observed during navigation.
rca of the issue: N/A – no functional bugs found in this iteration.
